text: After ten years of loyalty the company announced the layoffs by email, with a paragraph about gratitude and a PDF attachment explaining how to return their laptop by Friday
The landlord raised the rent by four hundred dollars and when she called to ask why, the voicemail said the mailbox was full and could not accept new messages
He watched the video of the city council meeting where they voted to close the community center and every single member smiled for the camera afterward like they had done something good
She opened the package and the replacement part was wrong again, the third time, and the return label was already printed because she had learned to expect it
The insurance company denied the claim with a form letter that misspelled his name and cited a clause he had never been told about when he signed the policy
Someone keyed a long scratch into the driver's side door while it was parked at the grocery store and didn't leave a note, just a silver line from the headlight to the taillight
The coworker who did nothing on the group project stood up during the presentation and used the word we as if she had been there for every late night and every revision
His brother borrowed the truck and returned it with an empty tank, a dent in the fender, and a text that said thanks man with a thumbs-up emoji
The referee missed the call and everyone in the stands saw it, the replay showed it, the commentator said it, and the game continued as though nothing had happened
She spent two hours on hold listening to the same forty-five seconds of saxophone music on a loop and when a person finally answered they transferred her to the wrong department
The contractor said three weeks and it took four months and the kitchen was still not done and every invoice had a new line item that had never been discussed
He found out from a coworker at lunch that he had been passed over for the promotion, not from his manager, not from HR, from someone who heard it in a meeting he was not invited to
The neighbor's dog dug up the flower bed again, the same bed she had replanted twice, and the neighbor shrugged and said dogs will be dogs while standing in his own pristine yard
The mechanic charged eight hundred dollars for a repair that took twenty minutes and when he questioned the bill they pointed to the labor rate sign on the wall as if that ended the conversation
They promised the delivery between 8 and 12 and he waited all morning and at 4 PM the tracking updated to say it would be rescheduled for sometime next week
The teacher called his son a disruption in front of the whole class because he asked a question that wasn't in the lesson plan
She read the article about the company dumping chemicals into the river that her children swam in and the fine was less than what the CEO made in a single afternoon
Someone cut in line at the pharmacy where she had been standing for forty minutes with a sick toddler on her hip and the cashier served them without a word
The HOA sent a violation notice because his trash can was visible from the street for six hours on collection day, the same day the truck came four hours late
He asked for help and was told to fill out a form and the form asked for information that could only be obtained by calling the same office that told him to fill out the form

The airline cancelled the flight and rebooked him on one that left fourteen hours later and the gate agent said there was nothing she could do while typing on a computer that clearly could do something
His ex told the kids they couldn't go on the camping trip because she had already made plans, and the plans turned out to be sitting at her apartment watching television

The parking ticket was on the windshield even though the meter still had twelve minutes left and the timestamp on the ticket proved it and the appeals process required a notarized letter sent by certified mail
Someone took credit for his idea in the meeting, almost word for word, and when he spoke up the manager said let's not get territorial about brainstorming
The hospital bill arrived six months later for an amount that was different from every estimate they had given and the patient advocacy line had a hold time of two hours minimum
She found out her data had been sold by reading a news article about a breach, not from the company, which sent a notification three weeks later calling it an incident
The restaurant served the steak well done after he asked twice for medium rare and the waiter argued that it was medium rare while pressing a fork into meat the color of cardboard
The scholarship committee gave the award to the dean's nephew and everyone on campus knew it and the announcement email used the phrase merit-based three times
He showed up to the interview fifteen minutes early and waited for an hour and when the interviewer finally appeared she did not apologize and spent the meeting checking her phone
The sidewalk had been broken for two years and the city kept marking it with orange paint and sending inspectors to look at it and nobody ever came to fix it
Her review said meets expectations even though she had exceeded every metric by double digits and when she asked for specifics her manager said it was a calibration issue across the department
The tow truck took the car even though the sign was blocked by a bush that the city had not trimmed and the impound lot charged by the hour including the hours it was closed
He sat through the entire two-hour meeting that could have been an email and at the end the organizer said let's schedule a follow-up to discuss next steps
Someone let their shopping cart roll into his car door in the parking lot and he watched it happen from inside the store through the window and by the time he got outside they were gone
The automated system asked him to say his account number and then asked him to enter it on the keypad and then asked a representative to verify it again when he was finally connected
She reported the harassment and HR said they would look into it and three months later the person who harassed her got promoted and HR said the investigation was confidential
The app updated overnight and moved every button to a different place and deleted his saved settings and the release notes said improved user experience
His roommate ate the leftovers he had labeled with his name and a date and a note that said please do not eat this, and when confronted said oh I thought that was old
The construction next door started at 6 AM on a Saturday with a jackhammer and when he checked the permit it allowed work starting at 7 and the foreman said the permit was in the truck somewhere

The bank charged a thirty-five dollar overdraft fee on a purchase of four dollars and eleven cents that would have cleared if they had processed his deposit first, which they received that same morning
The insurance company denied the claim for the third time using language copied verbatim from the previous denial, as if no one had read the appeal at all, and the hold music started again
